config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi agar tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menindas kelompok kecil atau suara yang tidak populer. Dalam pandangan saya, prioritas yang benar adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of statement makes me wary because it can easily become a justification for government overreach, majoritarian abuse, or silencing unpopular people. I do think individual rights are not absolute in every case, but the burden should be on society to prove a real, concrete harm before overriding them. In practice, a healthy society protects rights first and uses collective goals as a limit, not a blank check.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dimaknai secara mutlak, karena di Indonesia pun kepentingan masyarakat sering dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membungkam individu yang lemah atau tidak populer. Pengalaman kita menunjukkan bahwa atas nama ketertiban, moralitas, atau stabilitas, hak orang bisa mudah dikorbankan kalau tidak ada batas yang tegas. Bagi saya, kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibuktikan ada kerugian nyata dan pembatasannya harus proporsional, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas menginginkannya. Jadi, yang benar bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan menjaga keseimbangan dengan perlindungan hak yang kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American point of view, that framing is dangerous because it too easily turns into majority rule without restraint, and our history has plenty of examples where “the common good” was used to excuse discrimination, censorship, or government abuse. I do think rights can be limited when there is a clear, concrete harm to others, but those limits have to be narrow, justified, and accountable. So for me, the default should be individual rights first, with society’s interests serving as a serious but limited check.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Pengalaman di Indonesia justru menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi mayoritas” sering dipakai untuk menekan orang yang lemah, berbeda, atau tidak populer, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prioritas kolektif yang terlalu luas. Hak individu boleh dibatasi, tetapi hanya kalau ada bahaya nyata, aturannya jelas, dan pembatasannya benar-benar proporsional. Jadi bagi saya, masyarakat penting, tetapi hak individu tetap harus menjadi pagar utama, bukan korban pertama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general principle. From an American perspective, that sounds like a recipe for majoritarian abuse, because “the common good” has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. I do think rights can be limited when there is clear harm to others, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. In my view, a free society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the default and collective goals as something that must earn their way in.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
